brief_justification: Paper A's machinery is holographic AdS/CFT geometry: a defect extremal surface / island-formula computation of fine-grained entanglement entropy for an interface CFT dual to AdS3 regions joined by an end-of-the-world brane, controlled by brane tension and yielding Page curves. Paper B's machinery is classical stochastic population dynamics: repeated threshold-based truncation (harvesting) of an evolving probability density under normal/anomalous processes, producing initial-condition-independent quasi-steady states and an effective drift. There is no role-for-role correspondence between an extremal-surface/island entropy prescription and threshold removal reshaping a Fokker-Planck-type density; the only overlap is the generic word 'threshold', not shared governing machinery.
